title: LLM Architecture: Neural Net + Scaffolding
description: An LLM (Large Language Model) is fundamentally a specific type of deep neural network, almost universally based on the Transformer architecture. However, it requires non-neural-net components ('the plus some stuff') to function as a text generator. The core neural net calculates probabilities over a vocabulary, but it is surrounded by a Tokenizer (which translates raw text strings into numerical IDs and back) and a Sampling/Inference Engine (which uses algorithms like temperature, top-k, and top-p to decide which word to actually pick from the neural net's output distribution).
